preamble: The transformation from a voiced retroflex nasal /ɳ/ to a palatal nasal /ɲ/ involves a fronting of the articulation, where the point of contact in the mouth shifts from the retroflex position (tongue tip curled back) to the palatal region (tongue body against the hard palate). This change represents a shift in the place of articulation towards the front of the oral cavity.
phoneticEffects: Fronting, change in place of articulation

examples.note: A change of final consonant from /ɳ/ to /ɲ/ is noted in the context of a morphological fusion process, specifically in the potential derivation of 'banybili' from 'barn' (which would have contained /ɳ/) in Mudburra, possibly influenced by Jingulu.

sources: Hamilton-Hollaway, A. (2024). Paradigm Shift: A Theoretical and Descriptive Study of Mudburra-Kriol Contact. The University of Queensland.
